source_pr.title: test: use concurrent-safe structure to fix a flaky test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #42301

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: TestCheckPeersBusy was flaky due to racy cross-goroutine communication (appending to a shared slice) and a fixed sleep-based timing assumption.
failure_signature: Intermittent assertion failures in TestCheckPeersBusy (retry job not observed in time) and/or race detector findings due to unsynchronized shared slice access.
root_cause_explanation: The flake was caused by concurrency + timing: the test used a shared slice to record a retry job from another goroutine (not concurrency-safe) and relied on a fixed `time.Sleep` to assume the worker was sleeping before asserting. This PR stabilizes the test by switching to a channel for cross-goroutine signaling and replacing the fixed sleep with `require.Eventually`, plus using t.Cleanup to restore a package-level backoff knob used by the worker.
fix_pattern: Use a channel instead of a shared slice for cross-goroutine communication, replace `time.Sleep` with `require.Eventually`, and restore modified package-level backoff settings via t.Cleanup.

evidence: evidence: test: use concurrent-safe structure to fix a flaky test
